keyInsight: Chadd Hoffner is single-point-of-failure: 6 active + 11 archived jobs, and the two biggest active builds (Sunshine Kebabs, Karuah Internal) are both bleeding labour past budget right now.

recommendations.title: Sunshine Kebabs is repeating the Fenwicks Marina pattern — intervene now
recommendations.evidence: Sunshine Kebabs East Maitland (251206, $205k, Chadd Hoffner) is at 18.1% actual vs 22.8% planned with $37,036 labour overrun and 7 variations (9.9% of contract). That's the same fingerprint as archived Fenwicks Marina (251012, $78k) which finished 20.3% vs 27.4% planned, $8,376 labour overrun, 3 variations, 234 build days. Karuah Internal BUILD (250522, $176k) is showing the same trail: 17.1% vs 22.1%, $7,052 over, 4 variations.
dataPoints: Sunshine Kebabs labourOverrun $37,036, 7 variations
Fenwicks Marina archived at 20.3% vs 27.4% planned, 234 build days
Karuah Internal BUILD labourOverrun $7,052, 4 variations, margin slipping 22.1%→17.1%
Both active jobs are Chadd Hoffner BUILDs >$150k
recommendations.reasoning: SBI's biggest active jobs all carry the same warning signs that produced the only two materially negative archived margins on real builds. Variation creep + labour overrun + long cycles is the recurring failure mode on $150k+ builds.
recommendations.action: Freeze further variations on Sunshine Kebabs and Karuah Internal this week; do a labour-to-go forecast and lock a completion plan with Chadd by Friday.
recommendations.impact: Protect ~$10-20k of margin on the two largest active jobs before further slippage.

recommendations.title: Chadd Hoffner capacity is maxed — redistribute or triage
recommendations.evidence: Chadd Hoffner carries 6 of 13 active jobs AND 11 of 14 archived jobs, with avg build cycle 113 days and avg variation 3.6%. Every other PM has ≤2 active jobs. The two longest archived cycles in the dataset (Extend Rehab 333 days, Fenwicks Marina 234 days, Bozmik 229 days) are all his — and all three are the highest-variation jobs (9, 3, 4 variations).
dataPoints: Chadd Hoffner: 6 active + 11 archived
Extend Rehab 333 build days, 9 variations, $13,906 labour overrun
Fenwicks Marina 234 days, 3 variations
Other PMs (Leah Procter, Michael Cook, Gail Cook, Gemma King) hold 1-2 active each
recommendations.reasoning: Build cycles and variation counts correlate directly with PM load. Chadd is the bottleneck producing the long-tail margin erosion; spreading mid-size work to Chadd Yorston/Leah Procter would shorten cycles and reduce variation drift.
recommendations.action: Reassign at least one mid-size active build (e.g. Hungry Wolfs Ourimbah or Wyoming Medical) off Chadd Hoffner to Chadd Yorston or Leah Procter this fortnight.
recommendations.impact: Free Chadd to focus on the two $150k+ at-risk jobs; cut average build cycle by 20-30 days on reassigned work.

recommendations.title: Estimating standard of 23.1% margin is mechanical, not real
recommendations.evidence: Eight separate jobs carry an identical 23.1% planned margin: Taylors Conveyancing, Tracey Fletcher, Marine Rescue, Endo Water Damage, Erina Sawmill, Display Cabinet Adolphus, Hungry Wolfs, Lakehaven Post Office. Actuals range from 22.5% (Adolphus, near-miss) to 61.7% (Tracey Fletcher) — telling us estimating is using a default markup rather than job-specific cost modelling.
dataPoints: 8 jobs planned at exactly 23.1%
Tracey Fletcher actual 61.7% (gap +38.6)
Marine Rescue actual 54.1% (gap +31)
Adolphus Display Cabinet actual 22.5% (gap -0.6) — the only one that landed near plan
recommendations.reasoning: When the planned margin is a constant, every win is either accidentally underpriced (we leave money on the table) or accidentally fat. Tracey Fletcher's 38.6pt overshoot means we likely could have won a bigger scope; Adolphus shows the default is tight enough to lose money on the wrong job.
recommendations.action: Rebuild estimating templates by job type (small joinery, mid build, fitout >$75k) with separate labour-hour benchmarks; retire the flat 23.1% default this quarter.
recommendations.impact: Tighter quoting on small jobs (avoid Adolphus-style breakeven) and more competitive pricing on jobs we currently over-quote.

recommendations.title: $75k–$300k win rate has collapsed — investigate the mid-band
recommendations.evidence: Win rate by size band: <$25k = 51%, $25-75k = 43%, $75-150k = 29%, $150-300k = 23%, $300k+ = 44%. The mid-band ($75-300k, 47 lost vs 17 won) is where SBI is losing most badly — yet that's where Hospitality Fitout (avgWonValue $130k) and large Office Fitouts live. Meanwhile Search source converts only 27.5% (50 lost) despite avg won value $66k.
dataPoints: $75-150k band: 29% win rate (10 won / 24 lost)
$150-300k band: 23% win rate (7 won / 23 lost)
Search source: 71 deals, 19 won, $66k avg
Existing Client source: 68% win rate, $37.5k avg
recommendations.reasoning: SBI wins small jobs (relationship + speed) and the occasional big one, but is uncompetitive in the meaty middle. This is likely a pricing or presentation problem — small enough that procurement matters, big enough that SBI isn't yet differentiated.
recommendations.action: Pull the last 10 lost $75-300k deals and have leadership review pricing vs winner where known; trial a structured presentation/proposal format for this band.
recommendations.impact: A 10pt lift in mid-band win rate adds ~5-6 wins/year at $100k+ each.

recommendations.title: Two archived jobs have catastrophic data integrity issues — fix before reporting
recommendations.evidence: Mingara Club Small Office Fitout (251213, PM='PM', $88,668 rev) was planned at 92.3% margin with $0 labour budget, actualised at 27.1% with $20,818 labour overrun — a -65.2pt margin gap. John Dosanjh 45 Gordon (260509, PM='TBC') planned 100% / $0 cost, actual 13.8% — -86.2pt gap. Active job Lauren Moore Newcastle (260521) is currently planned at -25.6% margin, actual 100%, $10,065 labour 'overrun' negative.
dataPoints: Mingara Small Office Fitout: planned 92.3%, actual 27.1%, PM literally 'PM'
John Dosanjh: planned 100% margin, no PM assigned
Lauren Moore Newcastle active: planned margin -25.6%
Karuah Deck active showing 98.6% actual margin with $13k negative labour overrun
recommendations.reasoning: These aren't real margins — they're un-estimated jobs being passed off as wins. They distort PM averages (the 'PM' row shows -65.2% gap; 'TBC' shows -86.2%) and make leadership think the business is healthier than it is.
recommendations.action: Assign a real PM and re-estimate the four flagged jobs this week; bar any new job from being sold without a populated plannedCost and labourBudget.
recommendations.impact: Restore reporting integrity; prevent recurrence on Do-and-Charge and small joinery jobs.

recommendations.title: Peninsula Villages pilot is the highest-leverage active job — protect it
recommendations.evidence: Peninsula Villages (260511) is tagged 'Joinery — Retirement Village (Pilot Room — ongoing 60+ rooms)' at only $7,585 but currently running 41.4% actual vs 10.6% planned. If the pilot converts to the 60-room programme, it's a ~$450k+ annuity. It's under Chadd Hoffner, who is already overloaded.
dataPoints: Peninsula Villages scope note: 'Pilot Room — ongoing 60+ rooms'
Current pilot: 41.4% actual vs 10.6% planned margin
PM is Chadd Hoffner (6 active jobs)
Pipeline Joinery stage: 15 deals / $30k total — small ticket
recommendations.reasoning: The pilot's strong margin masks the strategic importance — this is the only active job with an obvious multi-room expansion path, and it's sitting with the most-loaded PM. Joinery pipeline value is thin ($30k across 15 deals), so this client matters more than the job size suggests.
recommendations.action: Have Chadd Yorston or Leah Procter shadow Peninsula Villages now; confirm rollout commercials and lock a margin floor before the second room is quoted.
recommendations.impact: Secure a ~$450k recurring joinery programme and de-risk continuity if Chadd Hoffner is unavailable.

recommendations.title: Existing-client channel is your best ROI — but top clients are dormant
recommendations.evidence: Existing Client source wins 68% at $37.5k avg (17/25). Yet the top historical clients — Wyong Golf Club ($611k, 1 deal), Banksia Paediatrics ($354k, 1 deal), Dinki Di ($351k, 1 deal), Geoff O'Reilly/Nash Allen ($267k, 1 deal) — show no repeat engagement. Repeat-client list is dominated by lost-tender entries (McDonalds Morisset 0/3, LAHC Adamstown 0/2, Awaba House 0/2).
dataPoints: Existing Client win rate 68% vs Search 27.5%
Wyong Golf Club $611k, 1 deal only
Banksia Paediatrics $354k, 1 deal only
Austral Herbs is the ONLY top-10 client with repeat wins (2 deals, $527k)
recommendations.reasoning: SBI's highest-converting channel is starved while the team chases low-yield Search leads. The biggest past clients have not been re-touched — every one is a six-figure account collecting dust.
recommendations.action: Build a 'top 10 dormant accounts' callback list this week; have a director personally re-engage Wyong Golf, Banksia, Dinki Di, Geoff O'Reilly within 30 days.
recommendations.impact: Two reactivations at historical avg = $300-600k of high-margin work at 68% conversion odds.

recommendations.title: Variation count is the leading indicator of margin loss — make it a gate
recommendations.evidence: Every archived job with negative or near-zero margin gap had variations: Adolphus Display (1 var, -0.6 gap), Fenwicks Marina (3 vars, -7.1 gap), Mingara Small Office (3 vars, -65.2 gap). Active Sunshine Kebabs already has 7 variations / 9.9% of contract with margin slipping 22.8%→18.1%. By contrast, zero-variation archived jobs averaged a +20pt positive margin gap.
dataPoints: Fenwicks Marina: 3 variations, margin gap -7.1pt
Adolphus Display: 1 variation, margin gap -0.6pt
Sunshine Kebabs active: 7 variations, 9.9% of contract
All 5 zero-variation archived BUILD jobs landed at +8.3 to +38.6pt gap
recommendations.reasoning: Variations aren't just admin — in SBI's data they are the strongest single predictor of margin erosion. The team currently treats them as ad-hoc add-ons rather than a margin event.
recommendations.action: Introduce a 'third variation' approval gate: any job exceeding 3 variations or 5% of contract value requires director sign-off and a margin re-forecast.
recommendations.impact: Catch margin erosion on big builds before it crystallises; protect 5-10pt of margin on multi-variation jobs.

patternsObserved.notes: Chadd Hoffner is the centre of gravity for delivery — 79% of archived jobs and 46% of active jobs sit with him, and the longest cycles, biggest labour overruns and highest variation counts all cluster around his largest builds. Estimating defaults to a 23.1% planned margin on roughly half of all build jobs, suggesting the quote engine isn't actually modelling cost; this hides both pricing-too-tight risks (Adolphus) and pricing-too-loose opportunities (Tracey Fletcher landed at 61.7%). The repeat-client list is alarming: the top four historical accounts by revenue ($1.6M combined) have not generated a second engagement, while the 'repeat' clients that do appear are largely lost-tender ghosts. Finally, variation count is emerging as a cleaner leading indicator of margin trouble than build days or job size — worth tracking weekly on active jobs.
